summary: Adversarial verification of every claim group in the Ep5 'Nonprofits + Farmers' season-finale script (draft 2026-07-01). 7 claim groups, cross-assigned so no agent verified research it wrote; the two farm groups were re-run after an oomd fleet kill (final versions are the ones embedded).
logs: 7/7 verified 2026-07-01: 5 accurate (fsa-microloans, freed-lsnc, connecting-point, close-recap, ucce-agdept), 2 needs_fix (eqip-nrcs, sierra-harvest) — ALL fixes applied same day

Headline catches: EQIP 75/90 are CAPS per 7 CFR 1466.23 → 'up to' anchors aired; Sierra Harvest grant is $745,627 federal per USAspending (not the org's '$750k+') → 'about three-quarters of a million'; org HQ moved per May-2026 990 → 'Nevada County' descriptor; 29-farmers stat attributed 'by their own count'

Capstone verified: Connecting Point = multi-county Joint Powers Agency (Sierra County BOS resolution names member counties Nevada/Sierra/Plumas) — 'public agency your own counties created' airs as-is; 24/7 = PHONE only
This episode's week-of pass is a PHONE pass — sierraharvest.org and ucanr.edu block automated checking from this network

fixDecisions.eqip-nrcs: 'up to seventy-five percent' + 'can rise to as much as ninety' (CFR caps, applied pre-wave-completion)
fixDecisions.sierra-harvest: grant → 'about three-quarters of a million dollars' (USAspending $745,627); 'Nevada City' → 'Nevada County food nonprofit' (990 HQ conflict); stat attributed 'by their own count'; office-location confirm added to phone pass
fixDecisions.notApplied: fsa rate wording stays 'around five percent' with the Aug-1 repost cue (verifier: if ≥5.5% say 'just over five percent')

hostAdLibBlacklist: Never imply free land — Land Match is matchmaking (lease / lease-to-own / work trade)
Never name Dan Macon as a UCCE advisor (moved regions; current: Olivia Henry, Andrea Warner — names deliberately NOT in dialogue)
Never mention Nevada County Grown (dead website) or KARE Crisis Nursery (closed Nov 2025)
Never hard-claim 'no income test for sixty-plus' at LSNC — phone-gated; the aired line is 'if you're sixty or older, just call'
Never call Connecting Point a nonprofit or charity (public agency / JPA) and never credit Market Match to them (Sierra Harvest runs it)
'Around the clock' applies to the 211 PHONE line only — text/chat have business hours
Never quote a hard FSA rate (reposts monthly; Aug 1 before record) or name an FSA office (unconfirmed — farmers.gov locator only)
EQIP percentages always carry 'up to'; never promise the grant number beyond 'about three-quarters of a million'

weekOfRecheck.note: Records Mon Aug 17 (day after the fair). This is a PHONE pass — run Aug 10-14.
pending: Sierra Harvest 530-265-2343: Land Match taking land-seekers? Farm Biz interest list open? costs/scholarships (air NO dollar figures)? current office (990 says 380 Sierra College Dr GV vs older Railroad Ave NC)
UCCE 530-273-4563 (Tue/Thu only): fall workshop dates, next Beginning Farming Academy, confirm S Auburn St address for the card
LSNC 530-823-7560: senior 60+ program funded this year + their own wording on the income test
FREED 530-477-3333: equipment reuse free + device list (ramps/hearing aids NOT confirmed — don't add)
Connecting Point 530-274-5601: 211 live-answer hours + Spanish before any ad-lib beyond the scripted line
NRCS 530-798-5527: EQIP CA cutoff dates; dial-test the line
FSA rate after Aug 1 repost: keep 'around five percent' or shift to 'just over five percent'
guest: Lindsey Pratt invite by MID-JULY (lindsey@sierraharvest.org)

content.verdict: CONFIRMED — nothing refuted; 2 flags: (1) the $60B stat's cited source URL has drifted (still defensible, swap the backup citation); (2) 'every episode lives at techempower.org/show' is TRUE-BY-AIR-ONLY — page exists and lists all 5 episodes, but zero videos are live as of 2026-07-01.
content.notes:
(a) FAIR DATES — CONFIRMED. nevadacountyfair.com (live 2026-07-01, daily-schedules page): 'The 2026 Nevada County Fair is August 12-16.' Fair is annually in August at 11228 McCourtney Rd, Grass Valley; the evergreen 'Every August, the county fair fills these foothills' survives replays. 4-H/livestock-barn color is generic-safe (site carries Livestock Exhibitor Info; hours not yet posted — irrelevant to the hook).

(b) $60B UNCLAIMED — DEFENSIBLE, SOURCE DRIFTED. The brief's citation (codeforamerica.org/programs/tax-benefits/) now reads 'As much as $15 billion in federal TAX benefits go unclaimed every year' — the $60B figure is NO LONGER on that page. The '$60 billion in benefits go unclaimed every year' claim still stands in Code for America's safety-net materials (Audacious Project grantee profile / Safety Net Innovation Lab coverage: 'More than $60 billion in benefits go unclaimed every year') and is bracketed by NCOA ($58B, older adults alone, 2023) and Link Health ($140B all federal benefits) — so 'more than sixty billion... every single year' remains conservative and defensible. Internally consistent with Ep1's spoken line ('an estimated SIXTY BILLION dollars', ep1-teleprompter.txt:163) and Ep1's fact-check report. ACTION: update the show's backup citation from the CfA tax-benefits URL to the Audacious/CfA safety-net profile + Link Health bracket; if pressed on air, say 'estimates run from sixty to well over a hundred billion.'

(c) FIVE-DOOR RECAP — CONFIRMED against the actual scripts. Ep1 = 'You Qualify for More Than You Think' (myth-busting opener) → 'busted the myths' ✓ (and the $60B line originates there ✓). Ep2 = 'Getting Connected' (internet + phones) → 'got you online' ✓. Ep3 = 'Food' (grocery money + no-paperwork help) → 'food on the table' ✓. Ep4 = 'Home + Transportation' (segments: energy bills / housing / the car / the bus) → 'kept the lights on and the wheels turning' covers segments 1, 3, 4 but OMITS the housing segment — not false, and arguably wise (Ep4's housing beat was 'the honest version': closed Section 8 waitlist, no open rent program — a recap line promising housing help would overclaim). Verdict: fair as written. Ep5 description matches today's content ✓.

(d) TECHEMPOWER.ORG/SHOW — page CONFIRMED live (fetched 2026-07-01): lists all five episodes (Ep1 'recorded June 2026, video coming soon'; Ep2 'coming mid-July'; Ep3 'coming late July'; Ep4 'coming August'; Ep5 'coming late August'), plus 211/findhelp get-help block and the 'Join our Discord' link (matches the script's Discord line). BUT no videos are published yet. The spoken claim 'every episode of this season lives at techempower dot org slash show' is only true at the late-August air date if Ep1-4 videos are embedded by then and Ep5 lands at/near air. PRODUCTION DEPENDENCY: Ep1 YouTube publish is still JP-gated (open task); Eps 2-4 record July 6 / July 20 / Aug 3 — the publish pipeline must keep pace.

(e) FINDHELP + 211 — CONFIRMED. findhelp.org is operational: archive.org snapshot 2026-06-28 status 200 (direct curl gets a Cloudflare bot-challenge — known crawler-blocking, humans fine; do not treat the 403 as an outage). 2-1-1: verified live earlier today — 211connectingpoint.org states 'OPEN 24/7 • FREE • CONFIDENTIAL', serves Nevada County / Tahoe-Truckee / Placer, and the county's own Housing Resources page names 211 Connecting Point as the Coordinated Entry access point — the season's standing door holds. 'English and Spanish' for the phone line: Spanish site nav exists but live phone Spanish is not explicitly documented — the script's existing [WEEK-OF RECHECK] phone-confirm cue (530-274-5601) covers it; keep it.

(EXTRA SCAN — unlisted assertions in cold open + close, all verified live 2026-07-01 on connectingpoint.org): 'public agency' ✓ (About: 'Connecting Point is a public agency...'); county-created ✓ (originally 'Nevada-Sierra Regional IHSS Public Authority'); 'since 2003... in-home care' ✓ verbatim; 'in 2011 it took on the county's two-one-one line' ✓ ('In 2011, we adopted operations of Nevada County's 211 program'); 'headquartered right here in Grass Valley' ✓ (208 Sutton Way, Grass Valley 95945, contact page); 'matches in-home caregivers' ✓ (IHSS registry); 'job help for parents on CalWORKs' ✓ (CalWORKs employment services/training/placement); 'FREE caregiver trainings and wellness classes' ✓ ('Choose from a variety of free classes on caregiving, health and happiness', cp-classes page); 'around the clock' ✓ ('24/7 access' on CP's About + 'OPEN 24/7' on the 211 site — the access-vs-live-answer nuance stays under the existing week-of cue). The script correctly avoids 'nonprofit' for Connecting Point and does not credit Market Match to them. No other unverified factual assertions found in the cold open or close.
content.scriptFix: No mandatory line changes — the copy as drafted survives adversarial checking. Housekeeping: (1) SOURCES DOC ONLY: replace the $60B backup citation (CfA tax-benefits page now says $15B tax-only) with CfA's safety-net/Audacious profile + NCOA $58B + Link Health $140B bracket; keep the on-air phrasing loose as written. (2) PRODUCTION CHECKLIST, not script: all four prior episode videos MUST be embedded on techempower.org/show before the late-August air (Ep1 publish is still JP-gated) — if the pipeline slips, change the line to 'every episode of this season is landing at techempower dot org slash show.' (3) OPTIONAL: Ep4 recap line omits housing — intentional-looking and safer left alone; if a nod is wanted: '...kept the lights on and the wheels turning -- and told you the honest truth about the housing list.' (4) Keep the existing [WEEK-OF RECHECK] cue on 211 Spanish/live-answer hours as written.

content.notes: ALL ELEMENTS OF THE CAPSTONE REVEAL SURVIVED ADVERSARIAL CHECK against live primary sources (fetched 2026-07-01/02). (1) LEGAL FORM + 'YOUR OWN COUNTIES CREATED': Sierra County Board of Supervisors Resolution (adopted 2024-02-20, official county record, http://www.sierracounty.ca.gov/AgendaCenter/ViewFile/Item/12376?fileID=13998): 'the Nevada-Sierra Connecting Point Public Authority is a multi-county Joint Powers Agency which functions on behalf of and discharges the duties of the respective members counties with regard to IHSS matters' and 'the three counties that form the consortium... Nevada, Sierra and Plumas counties.' Live About page (https://connectingpoint.org/about/): 'Connecting Point is a public agency,' 11-member Governing Board, 'funded with county, state, and federal dollars.' So 'public agency your own counties created' is exactly right, plural included — Nevada County (the audience's county) is a member; the Sierra angle is a feature, not a bug (the JPA name is Nevada-SIERRA; Plumas is in the IHSS consortium per the 2024 memo). (2) 2003 FOUNDING + IN-HOME CARE: About page verbatim: 'Since 2003, Connecting Point (originally the Nevada-Sierra Regional IHSS Public Authority) has been connecting people to In-Home-Support-Services and IHSS employment in Nevada, Sierra, and Plumas Counties' — the script's 'since two thousand three it's been connecting folks to in-home care' mirrors the org's own phrasing almost word-for-word. (3) 2011/211: About verbatim: 'In 2011, we adopted operations of Nevada County's 211 program' — script's 'in twenty eleven it took on the county's two-one-one line' matches, singular 'the county's' correct (it was Nevada County's program). (4) 211 COVERAGE: Nevada County, Placer County, Tahoe-Truckee (live 211connectingpoint.org service areas + iCarol record). (5) 'NOT SOME FARAWAY NATIONAL CALL CENTER': safe as scripted — the sentence describes the AGENCY behind the number, and the 211 contact center is Connecting Point's own program (iCarol agency record https://www.icarol.info/ResourceView2.aspx?org=2265&agencynum=28120626: operator CONNECTING POINT; About: 'our 211 Call Center'). The scripted line correctly stops short of claiming in-county live ANSWERING; keep the existing week-of guard against ad-libs. (6) CALWORKS: About verbatim: 'In 2014, Connecting Point expanded to provide employment services, training, financial assistance, and job placement for parents participating in Nevada County's CalWORKs program' — script's 'job help for parents on CalWORKs' is exact, including 'parents.' (7) FREE TRAININGS/WELLNESS CLASSES (current): site: 'Check out our free community classes! We offer caregiver training, health and wellness classes, and more' + cp-classes page live with in-person/Zoom/hybrid registration (https://connectingpoint.org/cp-classes/). (8) GRASS VALLEY HQ: contact page confirms 208 Sutton Way, Grass Valley, CA 95945; main 530-274-5601 / 866-577-6331; 211 access 2-1-1 or 1-833-DIAL211 (https://connectingpoint.org/contact/). CAUTION: Yelp still lists an old 350 Crown Point Cir address — use Sutton Way on any card. (9) 'MATCHES IN-HOME CAREGIVERS': fair — the IHSS registry page (https://connectingpoint.org/home-care/ihss/) says 'We maintain a list of caregivers to refer to seniors and people with disabilities enrolled in IHSS' and 'We will provide you with referrals... of Registry providers who best meet your needs' (consumer interviews + hires). Colloquial 'matches' is defensible; ultra-precise alternative if ever wanted: 'keeps a registry that connects caregivers with folks who need care at home.' (10) THREE-DOORS LINE 'free, confidential, around the clock, English and Spanish': STRONGLY SUPPORTED FOR THIS 211 — live 211connectingpoint.org banner 'OPEN 24/7 • FREE • CONFIDENTIAL' and '24/7 connections, 365 days a year, with a 211 call specialist'; iCarol record: 'Telephone assistance is offered 24 hours a day' (last verified 2024-08-16), languages English + Spanish + Language Line interpretation; site: 'Para obtener ayuda en español, llame al 2-1-1.' (11) HYGIENE: the Close (teleprompter lines 348-426) never says 'nonprofit' or 'charity' and never credits Market Match to Connecting Point; the existing [WEEK-OF RECHECK] block already guards both plus the live-answer ad-lib — keep it. BURN RISKS: (a) phone-confirm live-answer hours + Spanish line (530-274-5601) week of Aug 17 per the existing recheck block (iCarol verification is 2024-vintage; the site's '24/7 with a call specialist' is current but self-reported); (b) chat/text support is NOT 24/7 (chat 7am-6:30pm M-F; text 8:30am-5pm M-F) — 'around the clock' applies to the PHONE line only, don't extend it to text/chat if ad-libbing; (c) if anyone asks 'which counties?' on air: Nevada + Sierra created it (JPA name), Plumas is in the IHSS consortium — don't improvise beyond that.

Checked live 2026-07-01. WHAT HOLDS: (1) EQIP run by NRCS — 'USDA's soil-and-water agency' is a fair gloss (NRCS née Soil Conservation Service). (2) Practices all real EQIP work: NCRCD's live page lists 'irrigation system efficiency improvements, improving livestock distribution through fencing, forest thinning and pruning, conversion of brush fields to timber land or range' (the fire-safety brush claim); high tunnels confirmed on NRCS's own live EQIP page ('High Tunnel — provides targeted assistance… longer growing season') plus a dedicated NRCS High Tunnel Initiative with a California page — note high tunnels are NOT on NCRCD's local practice list, but are valid EQIP/CA. (3) Beginning farmer = 'operated a farm or ranch for less than 10-consecutive years' (NRCS advance-payment page, archived 2026-06-21) — script's 'first ten years' is fine. (4) Advance payment verified near-verbatim: 'provides at least 50 percent of the contracted payment for each conservation practice up front, before the practice is implemented' — script's 'at least half the money UP FRONT' exact; (unaired but true: must be spent within 90 days). (5) '$4M+ into Nevada County since the 1990s' — NCRCD live: 'Since 1997 NRCS has distributed $4.6 million to landowners in Nevada County'; script rounds down = conservative, safe. Figure's vintage undated on the page, another reason rounding down is right. (6) Card details verified on live ncrcd.org: '113 Presley Way, Suite 1 in Grass Valley', 'Valerie Bullard, District Conservationist (530) 798-5527' — but that's the partner org's page, not a USDA directory (USDA's locator is JS-only); dial-test the number week-of Aug 17. (7) farmers.gov/service-center-locator: 301s cleanly to the live 'Find Your Local Service Center' page — card URL good. THE FIX — the 75%/90% are CAPS, not flat rates: 7 CFR 1466.23(b)(1)(i) (eCFR, current 2026-06-30): payment 'may not exceed… Seventy-five percent of the estimated costs incurred'; (b)(3): historically underserved get the applicable rate +≥25%, 'provided this increase does not exceed 90 percent.' The current national NRCS EQIP page states NO percentages at all, and NCRCD's own local phrasing is 'NRCS helps to defray the costs by 50-75%.' So the regulation is the authoritative source, and it makes both figures maximums. Script's flat 'the government pays you back seventy-five percent' overpromises — payment schedules are fixed practice rates designed not to exceed 75% of ESTIMATED costs; a landowner's real-world reimbursement can land lower (NCRCD's 50–75% reflects local reality). Math note: for practices at the full 75% schedule rate, the HU bump (×1.25, capped) does genuinely land AT 90%, so 'rises to ninety' is defensible once 'up to' anchors the first number. Sources: ecfr.gov 7 CFR 1466.23 (API, 2026-06-30) · nrcs.usda.gov EQIP page (web.archive.org snapshot 2026-07-01 15:46, an hour before check — direct curl TLS-blocked from this network today) · nrcs.usda.gov EQIP Advance Payment Option (snapshot 2026-06-21) · ncrcd.org/partnership-with-nrcs (live) · farmers.gov (live). Bonus for the week-of list: CA EQIP Classic FY26 ranking deadline was Jan 15, 2026 — FY27 dates should post over the summer; the script's existing 'confirm cutoff dates with the office' note covers it.
content.scriptFix: One-word-class fix, two spots: 'the government pays you back UP TO seventy-five percent of the cost' … 'that can rise to as much as ninety percent.' (Optionally, Shawna could add the honest texture: 'in practice around here it runs fifty to seventy-five' — NCRCD's own number.) Card needs no change; dial-test 530-798-5527 (Bullard) week-of Aug 17.

content.verdict: AIRWORTHY — every spoken claim verified against the orgs' own LIVE pages (freed.org + lsnc.net + agencyonaging4.org, all fetched 2026-07-01); no script changes required; keep both existing [WEEK-OF RECHECK] items as written.
content.notes: FREED — (1) 'in Grass Valley' CORRECT: FREED's own contact page lists the main office at 435 Sutton Way, Grass Valley 95945 (voice 530-477-3333), serving Nevada County; the '117 New Mohawk Rd, Nevada City' address in older briefs is stale. Even the conflicting Yelp listing (2059 Nevada City Hwy, 'updated June 2026') is a Grass Valley street — so the CITY is safe either way. (2) NO-address decision STANDS: freed.org consistently shows 435 Sutton Way on contact + equipment pages, but the June-2026-dated Yelp variant means a possible recent move — phone-only on screen remains the right call. (3) Equipment reuse verified verbatim: 'refurbished medical equipment to seniors and individuals with disabilities free of charge', 'refurbished by volunteers' (supports 'checked over'), device list 'Wheelchairs, walkers, mobility devices, transfer boards, shower chairs, toilet seats, hospital beds, canes, and much more' — script's four named devices all on the page; script correctly omits ramps/hearing/vision (still unconfirmed). (4) 'disability -- any age -- or you're an older adult': page says 'seniors and individuals with disabilities' with NO age qualifier and FREED self-describes as 'a disability and aging resource center' — 'any age' is a fair read but not verbatim; low risk, already covered by the script's week-of phone check. (5) Statewide-referral claim VERIFIED: the equipment-reuse page itself links 'search for devices on California's Assistive Technology Portal' (myatprogram.org, the Ability Tools/CFILC exchange) — 'they can point you to California's statewide reuse listings' is a fair gloss. (6) 530-477-3333 confirmed (bonus: toll-free 800-655-7732, TTY 530-477-8194). LSNC — (1) Auburn office serves six counties incl. NEVADA (verbatim: 'Amador, Calaveras, El Dorado, Nevada, Placer and Sierra'). (2) Free civil aid confirmed ('free quality legal services' for 'low-income Californians'). (3) Every scripted case type verbatim on lsnc.net: 'evictions and lock outs', 'quality of housing' (habitability), benefits incl. CalFresh/Medi-Cal/SSI (Auburn page) + CalWORKs/SS/GA/UIB (what-we-do), 'debt collection defense', 'criminal record clearing'. (4) Not-handled list matches EXACTLY: 'Personal injury, Criminal defense, Divorce and child custody'. (5) Senior framing defensible and correctly soft: LSNC's OWN Auburn page says 'LSNC services for adults 60 and older are supported by the Area 4 Agency on Aging', and AAA4's page lists LSNC for Placer/Nevada/Sierra at 530-823-7560 — 'special senior program... just call' is supported; the script rightly never says 'no income test' (that stays phone-gated per the week-of note; OAA Title III-B means-test prohibition is the likely basis but is NOT verbatim on either site). (6) Phones verified: main 530-823-7560, evening intake 866-815-5990 (plus toll-free 800-660-6107 available). Ad-lib guardrails: don't add devices beyond the page's list; don't harden 'any age' or '60+ no income test'; don't say LSNC does bankruptcy/family law; evening line is intake-with-callback (1-2 business days per prior brief), not a live night lawyer.

ALL claims survived adversarial refutation vs LIVE fsa.usda.gov pages (browser-UA curl, 2026-07-01). (1) $50k/no minimum — VERBATIM: 'There is no minimum loan amount. The maximum loan amount for either Microloan is $50,000.' [fsa.usda.gov/resources/farm-loan-programs/microloans]. (2) Stripped-down application — 'Simplified Application Process: Designed to be more accessible and easier to complete than traditional' [fsa.usda.gov/resources/loans/operating-microloan]. (3) Audience — VERBATIM: 'financing needs of small, beginning farmer, niche and non-traditional farm operations, such as truck farms, farms participating in direct marketing... farmers' markets, CSA's'; 'Bees and bee equipment' an explicit loan purpose, so 'even beekeepers' is fair. (4) No land ownership — leases contemplated on the official checklist ('Bring copies of any written leases... if you are leasing land') and 'land rents' is an authorized loan purpose; no ownership requirement anywhere on page. (5) Apprenticeship/mentor — 'agricultural internships and apprenticeship programs, even those that are self-guided, count toward meeting the farm management requirement'; 'option of working with a mentor... FSA reviews the choice' ('approved mentor' = fair); 'not necessary... to have produced farm income'. Script's 'more than a napkin' framing correctly preserves the residual bar (page: applicants 'still need to have some farm experience' via those substitutes). (6) Business plan — 'a comprehensive farm business plan' required [operating-microloan page]. (7) Credit — VERBATIM: 'FSA does not rely on credit scores... expected to have acceptable repayment history with other creditors' = script's 'no minimum score, they look at whether you pay people back'. (8) Collateral — 'secured by a first lien on farm property or agricultural products with minimum value of at least 100 percent of the loan amount up to 150 percent... if available' — the crop/livestock/equipment purchased qualifies; script accurate. (9) Rate — LIVE TABLE TODAY: 'Farm Operating - Microloan 5.125% ... Effective as of July 1, 2026' → 'around five percent' fair; monthly reset confirmed. (10) Up to 7 years — 'from one to seven years'; nuance covered by 'up to' (annual operating/family-living money is due within 12 months or when commodities sell; only equipment/livestock runs to 7 yrs — no fix needed, but hosts shouldn't imply seed money stretches 7 years if asked live). (11) Purposes — page lists essential tools, fencing and trellising, hoop houses, bees and bee equipment, livestock/seed/fertilizer, irrigation, organic certification costs; 'feed, seed, and farm supplies' verbatim on the operating-microloan page. Full script list checks out. (12) Farm Ownership microloan — same $50k cap ('for either Microloan'); higher bar verbatim: '3 years farm management experience within 10 years of the application date' (1 yr substitutable), and the mentor path is 'not applicable to Direct Farm Ownership Microloans' — script's 'experience bar is higher' correct. (13) Lender-of-last-resort — VERBATIM eligibility requirement: 'be unable to obtain sufficient credit elsewhere, with or without an FSA loan guarantee' — the script's 'exist for farmers who CAN'T get a regular bank loan yet... program working as designed' is a fair, accurate framing of the statutory credit-elsewhere test. BURN-RISK / AUG-1 RATE FLAG: rates repost Aug 1, live at the Aug 17 record. Today 5.125%; May was 4.750% (drifting UP ~0.4pt/2mo). If Aug 1 posts ≥5.5%, 'around five percent' starts to shade optimistic — the script's built-in hedge ('resets every month, so check the current rate') plus the existing [WEEK-OF RECHECK] cue cover it; if the Aug rate is 5.5%+ say 'low-to-mid five percent' or 'just over five percent'. No dead-end risk found: no minimum-loan floor to disappoint small asks, lease path real, and the last-resort line correctly pre-frames the bank-denial prerequisite instead of hiding it.

Independent check 2026-07-01 22:20-22:40 PDT (sierraharvest.org itself Sucuri-blocks this network; all evidence below is my own fetches, not the researcher's). CLAIM-BY-CLAIM: (1) 'Nevada City food nonprofit' = CONFLICTED, needs fix. FY2025 990 (filed 2026-05-14, ProPublica EIN 90-1026798, https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/901026798) lists HQ at 380 Sierra College Dr Ste 110, GRASS VALLEY; Facebook page title says 'Grass Valley CA'. But the federal award record (signed 2024-08-20) and the live californialocal.com directory still say 313 Railroad Ave #201, Nevada City, and they're a Nevada City Chamber member — likely a recent move or dual offices. 'Nevada County' is the burn-proof descriptor; add exact office to the week-of phone pass. (2) Farm Institute whose job is launching farmers = INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED (The Union 2026-01-20 live fetch https://www.theunion.com/news/take-the-next-steps-in-farming-with-sierra-harvest-s-farm-biz-course-2026/article_e7a8a768-5a2f-4685-9745-061d5a3d8999.html; USDA award abstract; archived farmers hub). (3) Mentors who farm locally = CONFIRMED (The Union: 200+ hrs farmer-to-farmer mentorship since 2016; Farm Biz taught by regional farmers — FogDog, Sweet Roots, Feeding Crane, Starbright Acres; mentorship named in the federal abstract). (4) Grant coaching = INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED — the phrase 'grant coaching' appears verbatim in the USDA/NIFA award abstract on USAspending. (5) '$750,000 federal training grant' = GRANT REAL, AMOUNT OVERSTATED — needs fix. USAspending award 20244940043619 (ASST_NON_20244940043619_012), CFDA 10.311 Beginning Farmer & Rancher Development Program, NIFA, signed 2024-08-20, period 2024-09-15→2027-09-14 (active at the Aug 17 2026 record): federal obligation is $745,627 — NOT $750,000. The '$750,000+' figure is the org's own press release (YubaNet 2024-11-12, https://yubanet.com/regional/sierra-harvest-awarded-federal-bfrdp-grant-to-strengthen-farmer-support-in-the-sierra-foothills/) and is contradicted by the federal record ($942,019 total project = $745,627 federal + $196,392 non-federal match — no federal figure reaches 750k). Anyone checking USAspending catches the spoken '$750,000'. Say 'about three-quarters of a million dollars'. Bonus: this is their SECOND consecutive BFRDP (prior award 20214940035593, $476,355, 2021-2024). Grant serves Nevada/Placer/Yuba/El Dorado counties, so 'in our region' is accurate — keep. (6) Land Match = matchmaking, regular lease / lease-to-own / work trade, no free land = INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED from my own archive.org reads: program page (capture 2023-03-26) says 'helps connect farmers with local land-owners'; landowner intake form (capture 2024-02-24, sierraharvest.org/add-land-bank-property/) lists arrangement types verbatim 'Cash lease / Food/work trade / Lease-to-own / other' — matches the script exactly; no free-land option exists, so 'Nobody is handing out free land' holds. Newest full captures are 2023-2024 (2024-06+ captures are Sucuri 307s), and program is in the live 2026 search index; 'currently taking land-seekers' is already phone-gated in the week-of block — keep that gate. (7) '29 farmers / 100+ acres / 13 businesses since 2016' = PRESS-ONLY, SINGLE SOURCE — The Union 2026-01-20 carries all five stats verbatim, but they are org-supplied program numbers repeated by one outlet; no independent corroboration exists anywhere. Hedge with attribution ('by their own count'). (8) Landowners call the same number = CONFIRMED structurally (landowner intake form exists; 530-265-2343 is the org's only public number). (9) Farm Biz six weeks / online / budgeting + business planning = INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED (The Union: 6 weeks, Thursdays Jan 29–Mar 5 2026, Zoom, curriculum incl. business structure, budgeting and cash-flow planning). 'Runs each winter' = supported but inferred (Jan 2022 and Jan 2026 winter sessions in archives; not provably annual) — low risk given the 'get on their interest list' framing; interest-list mechanics already phone-gated. (10) 'Low-cost' + scholarship = scholarship application page INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED (archive captures 2020-2021 + live 2026 index); 'low-cost' rests on 2024 Ag Skills pricing ($22/class, $270/series, YubaNet) — Farm Biz price is unpublished ANYWHERE; the script already airs no dollar figures and the week-of block gates costs — acceptable as written. (11) Card sierraharvest.org / 530-265-2343 = CONFIRMED (live californialocal.com directory + archived site footer); phone re-verify already scripted. BURN RISKS RANKED: the $750,000 number (public federal database contradicts it) > 'Nevada City' (newest 990 says Grass Valley) > unattributed 29/100/13 stats (single secondhand source). Everything else stands.
content.scriptFix: Line 180-187 replacement: 'Two local doors, and I love them both. Door one: Sierra Harvest -- the Nevada County food nonprofit -- runs a Farm Institute whose whole job is launching farmers. Mentors who actually farm here. Grant coaching -- real humans who help you chase farm money. And that's not a brochure promise: the federal government gave them about three-quarters of a million dollars to do exactly this for beginning farmers in our region.' Line 203-206 replacement: 'Nobody is handing out free land. But since twenty sixteen, by their own count, they've matched twenty-nine farmers onto more than a hundred acres -- and thirteen farm businesses have launched out of that work.' Also add to the week-of phone pass: confirm current office location (990 filed May 2026 says 380 Sierra College Dr, Grass Valley; older records say 313 Railroad Ave, Nevada City).

content.notes: All claims verified against primary sources; refutation attempts failed. (1) 'UC's farm advisors — Foothill Farming, cooperative extension serving Placer and Nevada counties' — CONFIRMED verbatim from UCCE about-us page, archive.org capture 2025-11-07 (web.archive.org/web/20251107182557/https://ucanr.edu/site/foothill-farming/about-us): 'strengthen the local food systems in Placer and Nevada Counties'; site run by UCCE advisors (Olivia Henry, Food Systems Advisor). (2) Veterans Memorial Hall descriptor MATCHES 255 S Auburn St: UCCE's own page lists 'Physical Address: 255 South Auburn Street (Veterans Memorial Hall), Grass Valley, CA 95945' — UCCE itself uses the 'Hall' wording — and the county's official facility at 255 S Auburn St is the 'Grass Valley Veterans Memorial Building' (nevadacountyca.gov/830 and /1212; Yelp/Waze concur). (3) Hours Tue & Thu confirmed (8-noon / 12:30-4:30) + phone (530) 273-4563, same 2025-11-07 capture. (4) 'Once a year, two-day Beginning Farming Academy' — CONFIRMED verbatim ('Each year, we offer a two-day, intensive Beginning Farming Academy'), capture 2025-11-12 (web.archive.org/web/20251112173315/.../beginning-farming-academy); real past instance corroborated (placerrcd.org/event/beginning-farming-academy: 2-day Fri-Sat, Auburn, $80 meals cost). (5) 'Workshops/field days free or cheap' — weakest claim but unrefuted: historical pricing free-$40, 2022 two-day Academy charged only $80 meals; hedged phrasing deliberately chosen. (6) County ag dept — ALL LIVE-VERIFIED TODAY (2026-07-01, nevadacountyca.gov/1064, browser UA): Nevada County Department of Agriculture, 950 Maidu Avenue Suite 170, Nevada City ('up on Maidu Avenue in Nevada City' ✓), phone 530-470-2690 ✓, services list includes 'Farmers Markets, Field and Farm Stands and Direct Marketing', 'Organic Registration and Certification', 'Beekeeping & Apiary Registration' ✓. (7) County endorsement EXISTS: same live page's 'New Farmer Training and Resources' section lists exactly two orgs — UCCE Foothill Farming and Sierra Harvest ✓ ('these same two doors' is literally true; it also lists NCRCD seminars and UC IPM online training, which doesn't undercut the line). BURN-RISKS: all UCCE facts rest on Nov-2025 archive captures (live ucanr.edu WAF-blocks this network) — office days/hours/phone could have changed in 8 months, and UC ANR's 2025-26 budget turbulence adds churn risk to the annual Academy; the scripted WEEK-OF phone pass (530-273-4563 Tue/Thu) already covers this — do not skip it, and do not ad-lib an Academy date or strengthen 'free or cheap' to 'free'. Official facility name is 'Veterans Memorial Building' but UCCE's own page says 'Veterans Memorial Hall', so the spoken line is safe.
